title: React Testing Quiz
description: Evaluate your understanding of unit/integration/E2E tests, Jest, and the React Testing Library.

questions.question: Which and/or what is the difference between Unit and Integration Tests in React?
options: Unit tests are for strings, while integration tests are for numbers.
Unit tests isolate individual components/functions, while integration tests check multiple parts working together.
Only Unit tests can be used with React.

questions.question: Which standard JavaScript test runner is commonly used with React?
What is the core philosophy of React Testing Library (RTL)?
options: Test individual component state and internal methods.
Test components from the user’s perspective (what they see and interact with).

questions.question: Which function from '@testing-library/react' is used to mount a component for testing?
Can you use fireEvent.click() from RTL to simulate a user clicking a button in a test?
options: Yes, it fires the corresponding DOM event.
No, you must manually change the state instead.
Only if you return them with an ID.
